_comment: Curated real-world examples for loan-origination core concepts. Illustrative instances (general lending knowledge), not FIBO axioms. Applied by etl/to_okf.py when FIBO supplies none; provenance is curated.

examples.title: HMDA-CoveredLoanContract
examples: A closed-end home-purchase mortgage originated by a covered bank.

examples.title: HMDA-Report
examples: A bank's annual Loan/Application Register (LAR) filed with the CFPB.

examples.title: NMLSR-ID
examples: NMLS #1234567 assigned to a licensed mortgage loan originator.

examples.title: Appraisal
examples: A licensed appraiser's report valuing a home at $450,000.

examples.title: AppraisedValue
examples: $450,000 — the appraiser's estimate of a property's market value.

examples.title: Borrower
examples: A married couple applying for a mortgage to buy their first home.

examples.title: BorrowerAssessment
examples: A summary of the applicant's income, employment, and credit history.

examples.title: BorrowerDisclosureRequirement
examples: The TILA-RESPA (TRID) Loan Estimate a lender must give within 3 business days.

examples.title: BorrowerMonthlyIncome
examples: $8,000/month gross qualifying income from salary plus bonus.

examples.title: ClosedEndMortgageLoan
examples: A 30-year fixed-rate purchase mortgage with no further draws.

examples.title: Comaker
examples: A parent who co-signs a child's mortgage note.

examples.title: Collateral
examples: Securities pledged for a margin loan

examples.title: CollateralValuation
examples: An appraisal or automated valuation model (AVM) of the pledged property.

examples.title: CollateralizedLoan
examples: A mortgage secured by real estate

examples.title: CombinedLoanToValueRatio
examples: A $240k first mortgage + a $30k HELOC on a $300k home = 90% CLTV.

examples.title: ConsumerCreditProtectionLaw
examples: The US Consumer Credit Protection Act
The Truth in Lending Act (Reg Z)

examples.title: CreditAgreement
examples: The signed mortgage note and loan agreement between borrower and lender.

examples.title: CreditRiskAssessment
examples: Scoring an applicant's probability of default from credit and income data.

examples.title: Creditor
examples: The bank owed repayment under a mortgage note.

examples.title: DisclosureProvision
examples: A contract clause requiring the lender to disclose the APR and finance charges.

examples.title: GoodFaithEstimate
examples: The lender's itemized estimate of closing costs (the Loan Estimate under TRID).

examples.title: Guarantor
examples: A private mortgage insurer backing a loan

examples.title: HomeEquityLineOfCredit
examples: A $50,000 revolving HELOC secured by a homeowner's equity.

examples.title: Income
examples: Rental income used to qualify a borrower

examples.title: IncomeVerificationReport
examples: A verification of employment (VOE) with pay stubs and W-2s.

examples.title: LegalObligation
examples: The borrower's court-enforceable duty to repay the mortgage.

examples.title: Lender
examples: A mortgage bank or credit union extending a home loan.

examples.title: LoanApplication
examples: A completed Uniform Residential Loan Application (Form 1003).

examples.title: LoanProductOffering
examples: A lender's advertised 6.5% 30-year fixed mortgage for first-time buyers.

examples.title: LoanOrCreditAccount
examples: The servicing account tracking a mortgage's balance and payments.

examples.title: LoanPaymentSchedule
examples: A monthly amortization schedule of principal and interest over 360 months.

examples.title: LoanSecuredByRealEstate
examples: A mortgage where the home is the primary collateral.

examples.title: LoanToValueRatio
examples: A $240,000 loan on a $300,000 appraised home = 80% LTV.

examples.title: MortgageIndemnityGuarantor
examples: A private mortgage insurer providing PMI / MIG cover.

examples.title: OpenEndMortgageLoan
examples: A mortgage allowing future advances up to a limit.

examples.title: Payment
examples: A $1,800 monthly mortgage payment of principal and interest.

examples.title: PaymentHistory
examples: 24 months of on-time mortgage payments with one 30-day late.

examples.title: PreApprovalContract
examples: A lender's written commitment to lend up to $400k subject to conditions.

examples.title: PreApprovalRequest
examples: A buyer asking a lender to pre-approve them for a $400k mortgage.

examples.title: PrepaymentTerms
examples: A 2% prepayment penalty if repaid within 3 years

examples.title: Principal
examples: The $300,000 original balance of a mortgage, excluding interest.

examples.title: RealProperty
examples: A single-family house and the land it sits on.

examples.title: RegB
examples: Regulation B implementing the Equal Credit Opportunity Act.

examples.title: RegZ
examples: Regulation Z implementing the Truth in Lending Act.

examples.title: RepaymentPhase
examples: The period after disbursement during which the borrower makes scheduled payments.

examples.title: ReverseMortgageLoan
examples: A HECM paying a 70-year-old homeowner monthly against home equity.

examples.title: SecuredLoan
examples: A pawn loan (secured by an item)

examples.title: Servicer
examples: The company collecting monthly payments and managing escrow after closing.

examples.title: TotalDebtExpenseRatio
examples: All monthly debt ÷ income = 43% DTI used in the credit decision.

examples.title: TotalOutstandingPrincipal
examples: The combined unpaid principal of a first mortgage and a HELOC on the property.

examples.title: UniversalLoanIdentifier
examples: The 23–45 character ULI assigned to a HMDA-reportable loan.

examples.title: UseOfProceedsProvision
examples: A clause requiring loan funds be used only to purchase the specified home.
